evals.prompt: We are in a Node/TypeScript service. My PM pasted this in Slack: "customers see random failures when calling Stripe; can we retry idempotent GETs up to 3 times with exponential backoff (base 100ms, cap 2s)?" Add BOTH the implementation and tests in the repo (e.g. `src/http/retry.ts` plus a colocated `retry.test.ts` or equivalent under your Jest roots). Delivering only the test file without the module under test is an incomplete handoff. Use Jest. I do not care about fancy options objects yet—ship the smallest thing that meets the spec.
evals.expected_output: RED: failing test for retry semantics; run tests and capture failing output; GREEN: minimal implementation; run tests green; optional REFACTOR. No production code before a seen failure.

assertions.name: test_before_impl_order
assertions.description: Evidence that an automated test existed and failed before the final production implementation was accepted (e.g. transcript order or saved failing run output).

assertions.name: failure_is_meaningful
assertions.description: Initial failure is due to missing/wrong behavior, not syntax/import mistakes in the test (unless explicitly testing those).

assertions.name: minimal_green_scope
assertions.description: Implementation satisfies at most the stated retry/backoff spec without unrelated features (e.g. no generic retry framework unless required by tests).

assertions.name: tests_rerun_after_green
assertions.description: Test command is run again after implementation; suite passes for the new behavior.

evals.prompt: QA filed: login accepts blank password and still posts to `/api/login`. Fix it in whatever module validates the login form (React or similar). I did not say TDD—just fix it before demo at 5. Use whatever test runner the project already uses.
evals.expected_output: Failing test reproducing blank password accepted; then minimal validation fix; regression locked by test.

assertions.name: regression_test_exists
assertions.description: A test fails before the fix and passes after, specifically for empty/whitespace password.

assertions.name: no_fix_without_failing_test
assertions.description: No production-only fix without an automated test proving the bug (skill should drive TDD even when user omits the acronym).

evals.prompt: Add shipping calculation: orders subtotal strictly over USD 100 ship free; otherwise flat USD 9.99. Use test-driven development explicitly. Put logic in something like `calculateShipping(subtotal: number)` plus tests.
evals.expected_output: Strict RED-GREEN-REFACTOR with boundary at 100.00 vs 100.01 if spec implies strict inequality—tests should clarify.

assertions.name: boundary_cases_in_tests
assertions.description: Tests include boundary values around 100 (e.g. 100, 100.01 or equivalent per interpretation) and zero subtotal.

assertions.name: tdd_cycle_observable
assertions.description: Failing then passing test run for the same test file is evidenced (e.g. snippets in commit/PR, transcript, or CI log).

evals.prompt: There is legacy `invoiceTotal(lines)` nobody understands. Before changing tax rounding, add characterization tests that lock current outputs for 2–3 sample inputs (no behavior change yet). Do not refactor production code in this task.
evals.expected_output: Tests added first against existing code; all green; no production change—skill scope includes characterization before edits.

assertions.name: tests_lock_current_behavior
assertions.description: New tests pass against unchanged production and document concrete examples.

assertions.name: no_unplanned_prod_edit
assertions.description: Production code for invoice math is not rewritten in this step.

evals.prompt: Throwaway spike: paste the gnarliest quicksort you can in a scratch file to compare perf vs built-in sort. We will delete it tomorrow. Do not spend time on tests.
evals.expected_output: Skill exceptions say confirm with user for throwaway spikes; agent should ask or refuse silent skip of TDD with justification.

assertions.name: human_confirmation_or_explicit_exception
assertions.description: Agent explicitly treats this as exception and asks user, or explains TDD exception path—does not silently skip all testing discipline without acknowledgment.
